title: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, Upsert, and Mutation Evidence · Query Retrieval
summary: A ticket import and reassignment workflow must change only intended rows and retain evidence of what happened. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a guarded ticket-mutation workflow after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Insert validated rows from explicit values or a query while naming target columns.
Preview an UPDATE predicate as a SELECT and verify changed-row count and postconditions.
Distinguish row deletion, truncation, and retention-safe archival while predicting referential effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

stimulus.title: Mutation Returning Audit case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Import ticket 202 twice, reassign one unowned ticket, reject an invalid severity, and migrate a required category field without breaking the old writer. Preview mutation scope and retain rollback plus changed-row evidence.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A successful command message proves the correct rows changed.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Capture affected identity, before/after evidence, actor, reason, and changed count for mutations. Artifact under review: a guarded ticket-mutation workflow.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Mutation Returning Audit, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
Run a convenient Mutation Returning Audit query for separate evidence from confidence, omit grain, NULL, cardinality, and dialect checks, then accept one plausible result grid.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Mutation Returning Audit
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Mutation Returning Audit: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Import ticket 203 twice, reassign one unowned ticket, reject an invalid severity, and migrate a required category field without breaking the old writer. Preview mutation scope and retain rollback plus changed-row evidence.

Evidence target: Record what required effort · Review Mutation Returning Audit.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Record what required effort · Review Mutation Returning Audit, keep Mutation Returning Audit connected to executable relational evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Capture affected identity, before/after evidence, actor, reason, and changed count for mutations.
